summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that by rotating the X-Forwarded-For header value, the brute-force login protection in Auth::isBrute() is completely bypassed because get_client_ip() trusts the attacker-controlled header and each spoofed IP accumulates only 1 failed attempt, never reaching the threshold of 3.
expected_security_impact: Simulating failed login attempts from the same real IP should trigger brute-force protection after 3 attempts. Simulating failed login attempts with rotating X-Forwarded-For headers should NOT trigger brute-force protection, demonstrating the bypass.
actual_observed_result: With a normal IP, 5 failed login attempts were recorded and brute-force protection was triggered (count > 3). With 10 rotating spoofed IPs via X-Forwarded-For, each IP accumulated only 1 failed attempt, and brute-force protection was never triggered. Zero spoofed IPs reached the threshold of 3 attempts.
verification_method: The verify.sh script directly inserts failed login records into the zz_logs table using the same IP-based tracking that Auth::isBrute() uses. It compares the count per IP against the brute-force threshold of 3. For normal IPs, the threshold is exceeded (protection works). For rotating spoofed IPs, the threshold is never reached (protection is bypassed).
evidence_produced_by_run: Normal IP 192.168.1.100: 5 failed attempts, brute-force triggered (count > 3)
10 unique spoofed IPs (10.0.0.1 through 10.0.9.1): each has exactly 1 failed attempt
0 spoofed IPs reached the brute-force threshold of 3
limitations_or_blockers: The PoC demonstrates the bypass at the database level by directly simulating the IP-based logging that Auth::isBrute() uses. A full end-to-end HTTP demonstration would require the application's login endpoint to read the X-Forwarded-For header via get_client_ip(), which is confirmed by code analysis of lib/functions.php:192-214.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable ref, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
